case_title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al VS. RAUL AMILCAR VASQUEZ, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA et al

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF CELIA W LEE IN SUPPORT OF PLTFS REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORET OF EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL CORPORATION PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND THROUGH DENNIS J. HERRERA, CITY ATTORNEY FOR THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-01-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 21, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 IN ORDER TO PERMIT MR. VASQUEZ TO FILE A SUBSTANTIVE OPPOSITION ADDRESSING THE MERITS TO THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. (SEE ORDER FOR SCOPE OF INJUNCTION) PLAINTIFFS HAVE SHOWN THEY ARE LIKELY TO SUCCEED ON THE MERITS. DEFENDANTS DO NOT DISPUTE THE CODE VIOLATIONS. THE COURT FINDS SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE PREMISES ARE BEING USED TO OPERATE AN AUTO REPAIR BUSINESS, AND, EVEN IF THIS ACTIVITY HAS RECENTLY STOPPED, THERE IS NO HARM TO DEFENDANTS IN ENJOINING THEM FROM FURTHER ILLEGAL OPERATION OF THIS BUSINESS ON THE PREMISES. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SHOWN GRAVE OR IRREPARABLE HARM FROM THE ISSUANCE OF THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. EITHER PARTY MAY SEEK TO MODIFY THE EXISTING INJUNCTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)
ANSWER TO COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT VASQUEZ, RAUL AMICLAR

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-25-2013 Continued To Jun-26-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. NICHOLAS S. COLLA, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3819 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. VICTOR MARQUEZ, TEL. NO. 314-7831 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction is Granted. (See Order for Scope of Injunction) Plaintiffs have shown they are likely to succeed on the merits. Defendants do not dispute the code violations. The Court finds sufficient evidence that the premises are being used to operate an auto repair business, and, even if this activity has recently stopped, there is no harm to Defendants in enjoining them from further illegal operation of this business on the premises. Defendants have not shown grave or irreparable harm from the issuance of the preliminary injunction. Either party may seek to modify the existing injunction. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)
